metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about patterns (repeating, growing, number sequences, shape sequences, and pattern rules)

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Recognizing basic shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle)
Continuing a simple sequence (what comes next)
Recognizing simple color and object alternation patterns

questions.question: When we count by 2s, what number comes after 4, 6, 8, __?
questions.explanation: When we skip count by 2s, we add 2 each time. 8 + 2 = 10. The pattern is 4, 6, 8, 10.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding skip counting vs regular counting
commonMistakes.remediation: Practice skip counting by 2s with a number line

questions.question: Fill in the missing number: 5, 10, 15, __, 25
questions.explanation: We are counting by 5s. Each number is 5 more than the last. 15 + 5 = 20.

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice counting by 5s using fingers or a hundreds chart

questions.question: When we count by 10s, what comes next: 30, 40, 50, __?
questions.explanation: Counting by 10s means we add 10 each time. 50 + 10 = 60.

questions.question: Which shape has 3 sides and 3 corners?
questions.explanation: A triangle has exactly 3 sides and 3 corners (vertices). 'Tri' means three!

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused square (4 sides) with triangle (3 sides)
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Distinguishing shapes by number of sides
commonMistakes.remediation: Count the sides of different shapes

questions.question: Which shape has NO corners and NO straight sides?
questions.explanation: A circle has no corners and no straight sides. It is perfectly round!

questions.question: True or False: A rectangle always has 4 sides, with opposite sides being equal in length.
questions.explanation: True! A rectangle has 4 sides, and its opposite sides are always equal in length.

questions.question: Look at this pattern: 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, __. What comes next?
questions.explanation: The pattern 1, 2, 3 keeps repeating. After 1, 2 the next number in the repeating group is 3.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thought the numbers keep increasing instead of repeating
commonMistakes.remediation: Circle the repeating group and say it out loud

questions.question: What number comes next in this pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, __?
questions.explanation: Each number is 2 more than the one before it. 8 + 2 = 10. This is a growing pattern where we add 2 each time.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Finding the correct rule of a growing pattern
commonMistakes.remediation: Look at how much each number grows from the one before

questions.question: In the pattern Red, Blue, Red, Blue, Red, __, what color comes next?
questions.explanation: This is an AB pattern: Red, Blue keeps repeating. After Red, the next color is Blue.

questions.question: In the pattern Circle, Circle, Square, Circle, Circle, Square, what is the repeating part?
questions.explanation: The group 'Circle, Circle, Square' repeats over and over. This is an AAB pattern. Finding the repeating part is called finding the 'core' of the pattern.

commonMistakes.mistake: Only identified part of the core
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding the full repeating unit in a pattern
commonMistakes.remediation: Mark where the pattern starts to repeat and check if it matches

excellent.message: Fantastic! You already know a lot about patterns and sequences. You are ready to explore all kinds of amazing patterns!

good.message: Great job! You understand counting patterns and shapes well. Let's dive into the world of patterns!

partial.message: You know some pattern basics, but let's review a few things first. Watch the review videos and you will be all set!

needs_review.message: No worries! Let's first watch some fun videos about counting, shapes, and simple patterns. Then you will be ready to learn about all the cool patterns around us!

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We have some fun videos to help you get ready. Patterns are everywhere and you are going to love finding them!
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You are a pattern detective already! Let's discover even more patterns!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try again to check your progress.
